pi_items.source: task.json metadata and task setup script (setup_task.sh:11)
task.json metadata and task setup script (setup_task.sh:12)
DICOM file inspection and Hounsfield scale standards - (-1024 is standard minimum for CT scans)
Medical imaging literature confirms -1024 to -200 HU range is appropriate for air/paranasal sinus segmentation (sources: PMC8057972, PMC6239298, nature.com/articles/s41597-024-04176-1)
GitHub download URL: https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip - extracted dataset contains 108 DICOM slices organized in 0051 folder

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real CT Cranium 0051 dataset from the InVesalius 3 project (https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3), which is a publicly available CT scan of a human cranium with 108 DICOM slices. The dataset has HU range from -1024 to 3033. The target HU threshold of -1024 to -200 is appropriate for air space/paranasal sinus segmentation based on Hounsfield unit standards where air is -1000 HU. The DICOM data is real, not synthetic. All metadata values (file paths, threshold ranges, triangle count minimums) are consistent with the task requirements and medical imaging standards.
